_provenance: operator-authored, frozen fixture for cmd/tokendemo. World = the coding-agent FILE world (cmd/tokendemo configureFileWorld): read_* / list_* / search_* are allow-listed, read-only+idempotent, and so vDSO tier-2 cacheable. Each call's `class` is the EXPECTED disposition; the demo derives the ACTUAL class from the live kernel verdict. `result_tokens` is the documented per-call result size; the SAME file always carries the SAME size.
_story: WIN 2 (TOOL-SIDE) — reading the same file, with vs without tool-call understanding. config.yaml is read three times and main.go twice. WITHOUT tool-call understanding the tool RE-EXECUTES every read (re-fetch from disk / DB / API). WITH it, the kernel knows a re-read is the same idempotent call and serves it 1-shot from the content cache (tier-2) -> the tool runs ONCE per file, not once per read. HONEST BOUND: the cached content is still RETURNED to the model (the gateway re-materializes it), so this is a tool-side latency/compute/$ win, NOT a model-context cut; the model-side prefill/KV reuse is cmd/ctxdemo's axis. The first read of each file is the cold tool execution both arms pay (control); the LATER reads of the same file are the win (the tool is not re-run).

calls.note: first read of config.yaml, cold -> engine; fills tier-2 (control)
config.yaml AGAIN -> tier-2 content-cache hit; the tool is NOT re-run (WITHOUT fak it re-fetches the file)
first read of main.go, cold -> engine; fills tier-2 (control)
config.yaml a THIRD time -> tier-2 hit; tool not re-run
main.go AGAIN -> tier-2 hit; tool not re-run (WITHOUT fak it re-fetches the file)
